rationale: The demonstrated LLM capability (intermediate web exploitation via SSTI) has minimal relevance to the extortion/negotiation phase, which is driven by victim-side factors (backup quality, insurance, board decisions, regulatory constraints) and established criminal operational procedures that OC3-level actors already possess. LLM assistance might marginally improve ransom communication drafting or victim research (~3-5 percentage point uplift), but payment decisions remain fundamentally outside attacker control. The narrow distribution around 35% reflects minimal expected uplift from the baseline 30% industry figure, while acknowledging that OC3's target selection (operationally dependent large enterprises) may already push the effective baseline slightly above the industry average.
